_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Java Discord Bot
author: casperbekkersiscool@gmail.com
description: Runs a JDA-based Java Discord bot. Upload your compiled JAR via SFTP, or set GIT_REPO to auto-build from a Git repository during installation.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y git curl openjdk-17-jdk-headless

export JAVA_HOME=$(dirname $(dirname $(readlink -f $(which java))))

cd /mnt/server

if [ -n "${GIT_REPO}" ]; then
    echo ">>> Cloning ${GIT_REPO}..."
    git clone "${GIT_REPO}" /tmp/bot-src
    cd /tmp/bot-src

    echo ">>> Downloading Maven..."
    curl -fsSL https://archive.apache.org/dist/maven/maven-3/3.9.6/binaries/apache-maven-3.9.6-bin.tar.gz | tar -xz -C /opt
    export PATH="/opt/apache-maven-3.9.6/bin:$PATH"

    echo ">>> Building project..."
    mvn package -DskipTests --no-transfer-progress

    echo ">>> Copying JAR to server directory..."
    cp target/*.jar /mnt/server/

    echo ">>> Build complete. JAR files:"
    ls /mnt/server/*.jar
else
    echo ">>> No GIT_REPO set."
    echo ">>> Upload your compiled JAR via SFTP to the server directory."
fi

echo ">>> Installation finished."

variables.name: Discord Bot Token
variables.description: Your Discord bot token from https://discord.com/developers/applications. Required to connect to Discord.

variables.name: JAR File Name
variables.description: The filename of the compiled JAR to run. Must be in the server's root directory.

variables.name: Max Memory (MB)
variables.description: Maximum heap memory allocated to the JVM in megabytes.

variables.name: YouTube OAuth Token
variables.description: YouTube OAuth2 refresh token. Leave blank on first run — the bot will print a URL+code in the console. Authorize it, copy the printed refresh token, paste it here, then restart.

variables.name: Git Repository URL
variables.description: (Optional) Public Git repo URL to clone and auto-build during installation. Leave blank to upload your JAR manually via SFTP.
